metadata.summary: Select DOM elements, simulate user input (click, type, keyboard, mouse), handle forms, manage multi-tab flows, iFrames, dialogs, downloads, and mobile emulation in Puppeteer.

content.title: Puppeteer: Page Interaction, Forms & Advanced Patterns
content.summary: Covers all user-facing interaction in Puppeteer: CSS/XPath element selection, click/type/keyboard/mouse simulation, all form input types, multi-tab handling, iFrame access, dialog handling, file downloads, and mobile device emulation.

why.p: Browser automation scripts fail most often at the interaction layer — wrong selectors, missing waits before clicks, or mishandled dynamic elements. A disciplined approach to selectors (prefer attribute-based, not screenshot-derived) and targeted waits makes scripts stable across deploy differences.

list.item: Automating form fills, multi-step wizards, or login flows that require simulated human input.
Extracting data from pages that require interaction before content loads.
Testing UI components that open new tabs, show browser dialogs, or render inside iFrames.
Emulating mobile devices for responsive-site scraping or testing.
Pure HTML scraping with no JS rendering — use a simple HTTP client + cheerio, avoid the browser overhead.
Cross-browser interaction tests — Playwright covers Firefox and WebKit natively.

example.title: CSS selector and evaluation

rule.statement: Require the agent to fetch DOM via page.content() first and pick attribute-based selectors (data-testid, id, name). Never use selectors derived from screenshots — they are positional and unstable across viewport or content changes.
rule.rationale: Screenshot-derived selectors encode pixel coordinates or visual class names that break on any CSS or layout change; attribute-based selectors survive redesigns.

example.title: Click, type, keyboard, mouse
All form input types
Form submission with navigation

section.name: error-handling
section.p: Robust wrappers for navigation and element interaction:

example.title: Safe navigation with error classification
Safe click with retry
Multi-tab handling
iFrame handling
Dialog handling
Download handling via CDP
example.note: Page.setDownloadBehavior is per-session; agents that recycle pages without re-issuing it lose the download path silently.

example.title: Mobile device emulation
